questions.context:
[Situation: student checking with TA about lab report requirements]
Speaker: "How detailed does the report need to be?"
questions.stem: How detailed does the report need to be?

options.A: Well, just cover the main results clearly.
options.B: The lab closes at six on weekdays.
options.C: I returned my library books this morning.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking about gym or sports facility hours]
Speaker: "Why's the gym closed so early tonight?"
questions.stem: Why's the gym closed so early tonight?

options.A: The new treadmills are on the second floor.
options.B: Beats me, maybe a staff shortage.
options.C: I already finished my chemistry homework.
options.D: Yes, you can bring a guest.

questions.context:
[Situation: student checking bus or shuttle schedule]
Speaker: "Where's the campus shuttle stopping now?"
questions.stem: Where's the campus shuttle stopping now?

options.A: I usually just walk to class anyway.
options.B: The shuttle was pretty crowded this morning.
options.C: Actually, right outside the main library.

questions.context:
[Situation: friend inviting another to a study group or social event]
Speaker: "What time's everyone meeting for the study group?"
questions.stem: What time's everyone meeting for the study group?

options.A: I lost my umbrella somewhere yesterday.
options.B: That course has a really heavy workload.
options.C: In the study room on the third floor.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking about dorm maintenance issue]
Speaker: "The heater in my room isn't working again."
questions.stem: The heater in my room isn't working again.

options.A: Have you told the front desk yet?
options.B: My room gets plenty of sunlight actually.
options.C: The cafeteria changed its menu this week.
options.D: No, I don't have a spare key.

questions.context:
[Situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline]
Speaker: "Can't we still finish the project by Friday?"
questions.stem: Can't we still finish the project by Friday?

options.A: I left my charger at home today.
options.B: We'll pull it off, no problem.
options.C: The project is about renewable energy.
options.D: Over in the engineering building, usually.

questions.context:
[Situation: expressing concern about a friend's situation]
Speaker: "You've seemed really stressed out these past few days."
questions.stem: You've seemed really stressed out these past few days.

options.A: The weather's been nice this whole weekend.
options.B: Stress is a big topic in psychology class.
options.C: Yeah, midterms have been rough lately.
options.D: No, I've never actually been there.

questions.context:
[Situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space]
Speaker: "Couldn't you turn the music down a bit?"
questions.stem: Couldn't you turn the music down a bit?

options.A: That song is really popular right now.
options.B: I'm repainting my apartment this weekend.
options.C: The new speakers have amazing sound quality.

questions.context:
[Situation: student asking a professor about office hours]
Speaker: "When are your office hours this semester?"
questions.stem: When are your office hours this semester?

options.A: What day works best for you?
options.B: Right next to the main lecture hall.
options.D: The library keeps longer hours now.

questions.context:
[Situation: student commenting on the dining hall line]
Speaker: "This line for the dining hall's massive."
questions.stem: This line for the dining hall's massive.

options.A: I finished my whole essay last night.
options.C: The dining hall added a new salad bar.
options.D: No, I haven't eaten there before.
